systemInstructions: Generate a React + Vite frontend, Node local API, and workspace-local SQLite persistence.
Frontend code must call /api/* endpoints only and must never import SQLite, sql.js, database files, or filesystem modules.
Generate a React frontend, a Node local API, and SQLite persistence for a local customer manager.
The frontend must call /api/* endpoints only and must never open or import SQLite directly.
fileSystemRules: Write React and Node API files only inside generated/react.
Write schema, migrations, seed data, and app.sqlite only inside generated/data.
Do not read or write paths outside the active Sofvary workspace.
Do not create files outside the React + SQLite output contract.
Write React and Node API files inside generated/react.
outputRules: The frontend calls /api/health and /api/customers through relative /api/* URLs.
The Node API binds to 127.0.0.1 only and owns every SQLite operation.
Use the managed sql.js dependency or the Sofvary better-sqlite3 compatibility API only; do not add native SQLite packages or package install scripts.
SQL statements use prepared statements for user-controlled values.
The SQLite database file stays at generated/data/app.sqlite.
Keep Sofvary shell UI out of generated app source.
The generated app must be exportable without Sofvary floating menu or build overlay UI.
Use parameterized SQL statements for every user-controlled value.
The API must bind to 127.0.0.1 only.

acceptanceCriteria: Generated output contains exactly the React + SQLite project file set.
Frontend code calls /api/* endpoints and does not import or open SQLite directly.
Node local API owns SQLite access and binds to 127.0.0.1 only.
SQLite database file is stored inside generated/data/app.sqlite.
Every user-controlled SQL value is passed through parameterized statements.
Generated app uses no remote database, cloud service, sensitive credentials, external CDN, or Sofvary shell UI.
Harness sofvary.harness.react-sqlite@0.1.0 constraints are represented in the prompt envelope.
Before writing UI text, choose a concise software name. Use 'Timer' unless you can derive a better short domain name from the user intent.
Visible app title and browser title must use the concise software name, not the full user requirement.
Do not render PromptEnvelope text, runtime repair fallback text, diagnostic text, or Sofvary shell copy inside the generated app UI.
